edges.evidence_quote: Violence between the Bandidos and Comancheros continued until August 1984 when the two rival clubs formally declared war.
edges.context: The text describes a formal war between the Bandidos and Comancheros in 1984.

edges.evidence_quote: Hostilities between the Bandidos and the Comancheros resumed in April 2007 after more than sixty members of the Parramatta and Granville chapters of the Nomads , previously affiliated with the Comancheros, defected to the Bandidos.
edges.context: Hostilities resumed in 2007 after Nomads defected to Bandidos.

edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos members Michael "Kaos" Kulakowski, Saša "Sash" Milenković and Rick Raymond de Stoop were shot dead in the basement of a nightclub in Chippendale , Sydney in the early hours of 10 November 1997 by Konstantinos "Kon" Georgiou and Bruce Malcolm Harrison. Georgiou was a serving member of the Rebels at the time, while Harrison was a former member.
edges.context: Rebels members killed Bandidos members in 1997, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Police believed the shootings, carried out in Lurnea and Sadleir , to be linked to a dispute between the Bandidos and Rebels.
edges.context: Drive-by shootings in 2008 were linked to a dispute between Bandidos and Rebels.

edges.evidence_quote: Three men associated with the Adelaide chapter of the Gypsy Jokers – Robert Cameron, William James Fuller and David Shannon – were charged with Bobos' shooting.
edges.context: Gypsy Jokers members shot a Bandidos member in 2000.

edges.evidence_quote: A feud between the Bandidos and the Gypsy Jokers in the Hunter Valley began with several bashings before the home of a Bandidos member in Abermain was strafed in a drive-by shooting and a tattoo parlour owned by the club's Kurri Kurri chapter president Rodney Leslie "Pardo" Partington was firebombed in February 2001.
edges.context: Feud between Bandidos and Gypsy Jokers in Hunter Valley in 2001.

edges.evidence_quote: On 16 March 2009, a drive-by shooting was carried out at the house of Mahmoud Dib, the sergeant-at-arms of the Bandidos' Blacktown , Sydney chapter. Notorious is suspected of the attack.
edges.context: Notorious suspected of drive-by shooting at Bandidos member's house in 2009.

edges.evidence_quote: On 20 March 2009, relatives of Notorious members were the targets of two drive-by shootings in Doonside and Prospect . It is suspected that these were retaliation shootings carried out by the Bandidos.
edges.context: Bandidos suspected of retaliatory drive-by shootings against Notorious relatives in 2009.

edges.evidence_quote: Shane "Wock" De Britt, president of the Bandidos' Central West chapter based in Molong , was fatally shot in the head with a 12-gauge Franchi shotgun at his home in Eurimbla in retaliation after members of the Wellington -based Grudge Bringers gang were beaten and robbed of their gang " colours " during a fight with Bandidos.
edges.context: Grudge Bringers killed Bandidos president in retaliation for a fight.

edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos Brisbane chapter president Blair Raymond Thomsen, vice-president Ivan Glavas, sergeant-at-arms Kenneth James Whittaker and John Debilla, an associate and disgruntled former Rebels member, were convicted in June 2008 of firebombing a Rebels clubhouse in Albion , Brisbane on 27 March 2007.
edges.context: Bandidos firebombed a Rebels clubhouse in 2007 as retribution.

edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos are suspected in two drive-by shootings aimed at intimidation of Rebels members which were carried out in Bethania on 26 February 2017 and Calamvale on 10 March 2017.
edges.context: Bandidos suspected of drive-by shootings to intimidate Rebels in 2017.

edges.evidence_quote: Seven Rebels members were charged after Harley Cranston, a member of the Bandidos' Beenleigh chapter who was allegedly brandishing a machete, was shot and wounded in the leg at the Logan Hyperdome in the Logan City suburb of Shailer Park on 4 February 2019.
edges.context: Rebels members shot a Bandidos member in 2019 during a pre-arranged meeting.

edges.evidence_quote: Mark James Graham, a Finks member who would later patch-over to the Mongols , was convicted of attempted murder and was sentenced to twelve years and three months in prison in November 2014.
edges.context: A Finks member shot a Bandidos member in 2012, leading to conviction.

edges.evidence_quote: The kidnapping was allegedly a joint Bandidos and Nomads operation, as Sande was also affiliated with the Nomads.
edges.context: Bandidos and Nomads jointly conducted a kidnapping in 2006.

edges.evidence_quote: Lyle went on to join the Hells Angels and served as president of the club's Sydney chapter. In February 2011, he organised the defection of around fifty to sixty Bandidos members to the Hells Angels, which led to the dissolvement of the Bandidos' Parramatta chapter.
edges.context: Bandidos members defected to Hells Angels in 2011, indicating a shift in allegiance.

edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos were involved in a mass brawl with Finks outside a Broadbeach tapas bar on 27 September 2013.
edges.context: Bandidos and Finks engaged in a mass brawl.

edges.evidence_quote: A feud between the Bandidos and the Vikings Motorcycle Club began in May 1995 after the Vikings resisted a forced amalgamation by the Bandidos.
edges.context: Bandidos and Vikings engaged in a feud starting in 1995.

edges.evidence_quote: The attack was carried out in retaliation after a fight broke out between a Death Before Dishonour (DBD, a Rebels-affiliated club) member and a Bandidos prospect earlier that day
edges.context: Conflict between Bandidos and DBD member led to retaliation killing.

edges.evidence_quote: the Prisoners of War, a Hells Angels -affiliated prison gang, has been linked to the crime.
edges.context: Prisoners of War linked to shooting of Bandidos member Toby Mitchell.

edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos established their first branch in Western Australia by patching-over a Rock Machine chapter in Perth on 20 October 2013.
edges.context: Bandidos patched over a Rock Machine chapter, absorbing it.

edges.evidence_quote: The Perth Bandidos chapter patched over to the Rebels in June 2017 after senior Rebels members came to an amicable agreement with the Bandidos' eastern states -based leadership.
edges.context: Bandidos Perth chapter patched over to Rebels, indicating a transfer of membership.
